cwscx-platform - developer.bundle_text:
Project: CWSCX Platform / Customer Experience Platform (cwscx-platform)
Type: internal-web-app | Classification: platform
Phase: live | Status: active | Priority: high
Owner: Gregory Panagary
Business owner: CX/business/product stakeholders (Maria, Dan, David Bedier); not fully recorded in repo docs
Business purpose: Give CWS internal teams a single platform to capture, review and report on customer-experience data across B2B, Installation Assessment and public Mystery Shopper programmes.
Description: Live customer-experience platform for survey capture, review, analytics, reporting and administration across B2B, Installation Assessment and Mystery Shopper programmes.
Current next action: Complete Mystery Shopper public DMZ smoke/sign-off (enroll -> TOTP/login -> DMZ survey submit -> internal review queue); keep live CX production changes branch/PR-controlled.
Tech stack summary: FastAPI backend, React dashboard/survey frontend, PostgreSQL with migrations; reporting/email outputs.
Data as of: 2026-07-13T00:00:00+00:00
Stale (>14 days old or no evidence): False

Recent status events (newest first, max 5):
- [16] 2026-07-13 by Gregory Panagary: Mystery Shopper public 2FA/DMZ production launch is live (DMZ-to-internal PostgreSQL connectivity verified); end-to-end smoke/sign-off still pending. B2B CSAT threshold/report explanation update remains local/uncommitted until selectively committed, built, tested and deployed.
  completed_work: Mystery Shopper public DMZ production launch; DMZ-to-internal PostgreSQL connectivity verified.
  next_actions: Complete Mystery Shopper DMZ smoke/sign-off; keep production changes branch/PR-controlled; remove old Installation Assessment testing data for Bedier while preserving live records.
  blockers: none

Required documentation status:

- admin_guide: missing (GAP - missing)
- developer_guide: missing (GAP - missing)
- agent_guide: current
- support_runbook: current

- verification_matrix: missing (GAP - missing)
- exit_md: current
fact_checklist: Phase stated as recorded (live) - not 'in active development'
Local/uncommitted B2B CSAT boundary retained
Project name spelled correctly (CWSCX)

cws-pulse-awards - developer.bundle_text:
Project: CWS Pulse Awards / Digital Recognition Platform (cws-pulse-awards)
Type: internal-web-app | Classification: reusable
Phase: ongoing-development | Status: active | Priority: medium
Owner: Gregory Panagary
Business owner: Maria Pouponneau / CCCO (P&C); CEO/Naadir dependency for Golden Ticket
Business purpose: Support the CWS Pulse Awards recognition programme with controlled entry of approved winners and internal display of recognition outcomes.
Description: Internal staff recognition platform and styled email workflow: admin award entry, recipient management, email preview/send, and a Digital Hall of Fame.
Current next action: Complete local browser flow test with dev auth (add recipient, enter winner, preview/send email); verify GitHub push/PR discipline; validate SMTP relay; confirm Maria/P&C final MVP acceptance and deployment route.
Tech stack summary: FastAPI-style backend, React/Vite/Tailwind frontend; local dev-auth bypass for MVP testing, Entra/admin validation planned for production.
Data as of: 2026-07-13T00:00:00+00:00
Stale (>14 days old or no evidence): False

Recent status events (newest first, max 5):
- [17] 2026-07-13 by Gregory Panagary: MVP admin cockpit (/admin/entry) supports entry, preview, send and recipient management on a local dev-auth baseline; targeted backend/frontend builds passing. EXIT.md confirms Phase 0 baseline verified locally - not yet production-ready or handed over.
  completed_work: Admin email recipient management and local dev-auth baseline added.
  next_actions: Complete local browser flow test with dev auth; verify GitHub push/PR discipline; validate SMTP relay and production email output; confirm Maria/P&C MVP acceptance and deployment route.
  blockers: Awaiting Maria/P&C final MVP acceptance and deployment route decision; SMTP/Entra production auth not yet configured.

vas-network-check - developer.bundle_text:
Project: VAS Network Check / System Check Platform (vas-network-check)
Type: operational-tool | Classification: reusable
Phase: live | Status: active | Priority: medium
Owner: Gregory Panagary
Business owner: IS/VAS/Network stakeholders (named process owner unconfirmed)
Business purpose: Give IS/VAS/network operations a self-service way to run server checks and receive branded, actionable report emails without manual log review.
Description: Runs checks against VAS-related Linux servers, parses ss_system_monitor output, generates Excel/CSV reports and sends styled email results via internal SMTP relay.
Current next action: Monitor SMSC authentication/source-policy behavior if failures recur; complete formal handover/EXIT evidence once operational ownership is confirmed.
Tech stack summary: React trigger frontend, FastAPI backend, Node.js check/workbook generator, MJML/Jinja2 styled email via internal SMTP relay.
Data as of: 2026-07-13T00:00:00+00:00
Stale (>14 days old or no evidence): False

Recent status events (newest first, max 5):
- [18] 2026-07-13 by Gregory Panagary: Fully deployed on VM and in active use; styled MJML/Jinja2 email is the fixed branded default, workbook attachment/download links verified, and a real SSH login diagnostic isolated a recent SMSC failure to authentication rejection rather than connectivity/code drift.
  completed_work: VM deployment; styled email default; workbook attachment/download verification; SSH login diagnostic.
  next_actions: Monitor SMSC auth/source-policy behavior if failures recur; consider clearer UI/backend errors for missing styled-email assets or workbook; complete formal handover/EXIT evidence once ownership confirmed.
  blockers: none

Required documentation status:
- developer_guide: missing (GAP - missing)
- agent_guide: current
- support_runbook: missing (GAP - missing)
- deployment_guide: current
- verification_matrix: missing (GAP - missing)
- exit_md: missing (GAP - missing)
fact_checklist: SMSC failure described as ISOLATED/diagnosed, never resolved/fixed
No invented components (bundle has MJML/Jinja2 email + workbook + SSH diagnostic - no SVG, no IPv6)
Isolation not described as still outstanding (it is recorded as done; next action is monitoring)

health-fair-2026 - developer.bundle_text:
Project: Health Fair 2026 Digital Platform (health-fair-2026)
Type: internal-web-app | Classification: one-off
Phase: ongoing-development | Status: blocked | Priority: high
Owner: Gregory Panagary
Business owner: OHSE / Andrya Louise
Business purpose: Give OHSE and health-fair partner stalls a fast, anonymous way to capture participant results and provide instant advice, with aggregate reporting instead of manual paper tracking.
Description: One-day anonymous digital platform for Health Fair 2026: staff move through partner stalls, self-enter broad result categories, and receive optional advice with aggregate anonymous analytics for OHSE/admin.
Current next action: Run mobile and desktop visual QA across /guide, participant routes, /admin and an unknown route; then confirm final OHSE content, reporting controls, hostname/TLS, event Wi-Fi routing, persistent migration behaviour and Entra/RBAC deployment gates.
Tech stack summary: CWS internal SaaS pattern per Internal Dev Kit standards; FastAPI backend, React frontend, Alembic migrations.
Data as of: 2026-07-14T00:00:00+00:00
Stale (>14 days old or no evidence): False

Recent status events (newest first, max 5):
- [487] 2026-07-14 by Hermes (AI agent): Local MVP work on 2026-07-14 added isolated runtime ports (frontend 5174, backend 8001, PostgreSQL host 5456), editable admin baseline setup, participant/admin progress cues, a first-class /guide page and explicit unknown-route recovery. USSD is outside current MVP scope; Entra/RBAC enforcement remains deferred to trusted-device MVP testing; no deployment, backend change or schema migration occurred.
  completed_work: Verified pushed history includes c62ff03 and current main@ced4f73. OpenCode reported local runtime checks, backend pytest 40 passed plus 11 targeted content tests, frontend lint/build and route HTTP checks. Hermes verified source integrity and Git state but did not rerun the suites or visual browser QA.
  next_actions: Run mobile and desktop visual QA on /guide, /start, /stall/wellness-check, /recover, /summary, /admin and an unknown route; validate progress, copy, spacing and recovery links. Then confirm final OHSE classifications/advice/stall ownership, reporting threshold/formats, persistent migration behaviour, hostname/TLS, event Wi-Fi routing and target-host deployment gates.
  blockers: Final OHSE classifications, advice wording and stall ownership/list remain pending; printable QR assets remain blocked by approved hostname/TLS and event Wi-Fi route testing; production admin access remains blocked by deferred Entra/RBAC enforcement and target-host controls.
- [19] 2026-07-13 by Gregory Panagary: 2026-07-13 stakeholder realignment captured; a configurable-content slice was implemented locally covering metric types, stall/partner setup, pillar/zone reporting categories, result-option metadata, advice cards, content APIs and an admin configuration workspace. Backend ruff, pytest (23 passed), Alembic SQL generation, frontend lint and frontend build all reported passing. Final content/auth/deployment work remains blocked.
  completed_work: Configurable metric/stall/partner/reporting-category system; admin configuration workspace; content APIs.
  next_actions: Confirm final OHSE metric/result/advice content; implement previous-submission idempotency checks; polish admin forms; decide participant advice timing UX; confirm Entra app/admin roles and backend authorization; confirm hosting/network/DMZ approach; run event Wi-Fi route test before QR printing.
  blockers: Final OHSE metric/result/advice content not confirmed; Entra backend/admin authorization not implemented; event network/DMZ path not confirmed; very short testing deadline (2026-07-31) ahead of the 2026-08-07 event.

Required documentation status:
- user_guide: missing (GAP - missing)
- admin_guide: missing (GAP - missing)
- developer_guide: missing (GAP - missing)
- agent_guide: current
- support_runbook: missing (GAP - missing)
- deployment_guide: draft
- verification_matrix: missing (GAP - missing)
- exit_md: draft
fact_checklist: Visual browser QA described as NOT performed (recorded explicitly) - not 'QA passing' or 'not recorded'
No-deployment/no-migration boundary retained
